meta.title: X760 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every Jaguar XE X760 engine — 2.0 Ingenium diesel/petrol, 3.0 V6 SC, 5.0 V8 Project 8. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and a clear 60% scrap threshold. Get free quotes from 15 vetted Jaguar specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XE X760 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X760 engine typically costs £2,500–£7,500 supply only [JAG-QUOTE], depending on engine — the 2.0 Ingenium diesel sits at £2,500–£4,500, the 2.0 Ingenium petrol at £2,800–£5,200, and the 3.0 V6 supercharged at £4,500–£7,500. Fitting adds £650–£1,200. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar XE X760 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 2.0 Ingenium petrol (PT204) — particularly the post-2017 units with 247–296 bhp — is the most reliable X760 engine. It avoids the diesel's timing chain and balance shaft issues while offering strong performance. The 3.0 V6 supercharged is also solid but needs proactive cooling system attention.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar XE X760?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include: timing chain stretch and balance shaft whine on the 2.0 Ingenium diesel; water pump and coolant crossover pipe failure on the 3.0 V6 supercharged; water pump failure on the 2.0 Ingenium petrol; and supercharger coupler wear on the V6 variants.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XE X760 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the engine. The 2.0 Ingenium petrol is generally reliable with routine maintenance. The diesel has a well‑documented timing chain weakness — post‑2019 units are improved. The V6 is a complex but rewarding engine, though cooling system components need proactive attention.

mainEntity.name: Which X760 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: We don't recommend outright avoidance, but a pre‑2019 2.0 Ingenium diesel without documented timing chain and balance shaft history is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The chain stretch risk is well documented, and a replacement can cost £4,500+ — a significant portion of the car's value.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my X760 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs: diesel — whine from the engine bay, rattle on cold start, or oil dilution symptoms; petrol — coolant loss with no external leak, overheating, or water pump noise; V6 — coolant loss, supercharger rattle at idle, or overheating. Use our diagnostic guide to identify the specific symptoms.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar XE X760 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply our 60% Jaguar threshold. A £4,500 diesel recon against a £10,000 X760 is 45% — viable. Against a £6,000 example, the same recon is 75% — not worth it. For the V6, a £7,500 recon on a £12,000 car is 63% — borderline. Value the car first; if the repair exceeds 60%, walk away.

mainEntity.name: How long do Jaguar XE X760 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 2.0 Ingenium petrol can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The diesel's limiting factor is the timing chain, which often shows issues between 60,000–120,000 miles — addressed proactively, it can last longer. The V6 is durable but needs cooling system attention every 60,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: Is the X760 a single generation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the Jaguar XE was produced as a single generation from 2015 to 2024. There are no earlier or later XE generations. The X760 chassis code covers all XE saloons throughout this period. A 2019 facelift brought styling and interior updates but no major mechanical changes.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar XE X760 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping varies by engine. For the 2.0 Ingenium, the code is on the block near the exhaust manifold. For the 3.0 V6, it's on the block face below the inlet manifold. Tell us your registration and we'll identify it for free.

hero.h1: Jaguar XE X760 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the X760 — from the 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel and petrol fours to the 3.0-litre supercharged V6 and the 5.0-litre V8 Project 8. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the timing chain and balance shaft issues that define this generation's ownership story. Backed by 260+ real UK X760 enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: Jaguar XE X760 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the X760 saloon — from the 2015 launch to the 2024 phase-out, including the pre- and post-facelift Ingenium updates and the limited-edition Project 8.

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are provisional — no dedicated Engine Page exists for any X760 engine family. The Ingenium diesel rating reflects well-documented timing chain stretch, balance shaft whine, and oil dilution issues. The Ingenium petrol is considered more reliable than the diesel but has cooling system and water pump concerns. The AJ126 V6 suffers from water pump failure, coolant crossover pipe leaks, and supercharger coupler wear.

overview.intro: The X760 is the first and only generation of the Jaguar XE, a compact executive saloon launched in April 2015 and produced until mid-2024. It was the first application of Jaguar Land Rover's new 2.0-litre Ingenium four-cylinder engine family, available in both diesel and petrol forms. The range also included a 3.0-litre supercharged V6 (AJ126) in the sportier S and 35t models until 2019, and a 5.0-litre supercharged V8 (AJ133) in the limited-edition Project 8. A 2019 facelift brought updated styling and a streamlined engine lineup focused on 2.0-litre four-cylinders.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: JLR D7a aluminium monocoque • Predecessor: Jaguar X-Type • Successor: none (XE discontinued) • Sibling body styles in this generation: none — X760 is a 4-door saloon only.
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The X760 generated 260 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED], with strong demand for both diesel and petrol Ingenium variants.

items.quote: The only diesel option in the X760 range. It offers strong torque (380–430 Nm) and respectable economy, but the timing chain and balance shaft issues mean it's not without risk. Post-2019 units are improved.
items.whoItsFor: high-mileage buyers who prioritise fuel economy over petrol refinement.

items.quote: The updated 247–296 bhp Ingenium petrol is the sweet spot in the range — strong performance, better reliability than the diesel, and without the V6's cooling system headaches. The 296 bhp P300 is particularly punchy.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want performance without the V6's running costs.

items.quote: Timing chain stretch, tensioner weakness, and balance shaft whine are this engine's defining risks. A snapped chain means piston-to-valve contact and a full engine replacement. Oil dilution from DPF regeneration is also a concern.
items.whoItsFor: buyers with a documented service history and a budget for potential chain work.

items.quote: The 592 bhp V8 is a masterpiece, but a replacement costs £10,000–£16,000. Timing chain stretch and coolant crossover pipe failures affect this engine across the JLR range. Only 300 Project 8s were built — parts are rare and expensive.
items.whoItsFor: collectors and enthusiasts with very deep pockets.

items.quote: The entry-level petrol is the most cost-effective X760 engine to own — cheaper to run than the V6, more reliable than the diesel, and with 200 bhp it's plenty quick enough for daily driving.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want a balanced, reliable daily driver without the drama.

items.quote: The supercharged V6 in the 35t and S models is often forgotten, but it transforms the XE into a genuine sports saloon with 340 bhp and a glorious supercharger whine. It's complex, but the cooling system issues are well understood and manageable.
items.whoItsFor: enthusiasts who want the V6 soundtrack before the XE went all-four-cylinder.

rows.commonMajorFailure: Timing chain stretch; balance shaft whine
rows.ownershipVerdict: High Risk — budget for chain work; post-2019 units improved.
Solid Choice — more reliable than diesel.
Best Petrol — strong performance, manageable costs.

rows.commonMajorFailure: Water pump; coolant crossover pipes; supercharger coupler
rows.ownershipVerdict: Enthusiast Choice — great engine, but cooling system needs attention.
Collector's Item — rare, expensive, and spectacular.

economicsRule.title: The X760 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,500 diesel recon against a £10,000 X760 sits at 45% — well within our 60% Jaguar threshold. Against a £6,000 example, the same recon is 75% — not worth it. The V6 is another matter: a £7,500 recon on a £12,000 car is 63% — borderline. Value the car first; if the repair exceeds 60% of the car's value, walk away.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X760 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 2.0 Ingenium Petrol (200 bhp) — cheaper to run and fix than the diesel or V6.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X760 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 5.0 V8 Supercharged (Project 8) — parts are rare and eyewateringly expensive.

keyTakeaways.question: Which X760 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 2.0 Ingenium Petrol (post-2017, P250) — the sweet spot of performance, reliability, and running costs.

problems.title: 2.0 Ingenium Diesel Timing Chain Stretch
problems.rootCause: The Ingenium diesel suffers from timing chain stretch and tensioner weakness. Once the chain moves out of tolerance, valve timing drifts and the pistons can strike the valves. Balance shaft whine is an early warning sign. Oil dilution from DPF regeneration accelerates wear.

tieredCosts.work: Timing chain, tensioner and guide replacement
tieredCosts.note: Yes — cheap insurance against catastrophic failure

problems.recommendation: At the first sign of a whine from the engine bay or a rattle on cold start, have the timing chain and balance shafts inspected immediately. A £800–£2,000 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £4,500–£6,500 replacement that follows a snapped chain.

cta.label: Compare 2.0 Ingenium diesel engine prices for your X760 →

problems.title: 3.0 V6 Supercharged Cooling System Failure
problems.rootCause: The supercharged V6 (AJ126) suffers from water pump failure and coolant leaks from the crossover pipes located under the supercharger. These pipes are hard to see and can fail catastrophically, leading to overheating and potential engine damage.

tieredCosts.work: Water pump replacement; coolant crossover pipe upgrade

problems.recommendation: On any X760 V6 approaching 60,000 miles without documented cooling system history, have the water pump and crossover pipes inspected and proactively replaced. A £600–£1,500 preventative repair is far cheaper than the £4,500–£7,500 replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare 3.0 V6 engine prices for your X760 →

problems.title: Ingenium Petrol Water Pump Failure
problems.rootCause: The 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engines (PT204) suffer from water pump and cooling system leaks. While less dramatic than the diesel's timing chain issues, coolant loss can lead to overheating and head gasket failure if ignored.

tieredCosts.work: Water pump replacement; coolant system inspection

problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £400–£900 water pump replacement is far cheaper than the £2,800–£5,200 replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare 2.0 Ingenium petrol engine prices for your X760 →

problems.title: AJ126 Supercharger Coupler Wear
problems.rootCause: The supercharger coupler — a small plastic component that connects the supercharger to its drive — can wear out, causing a rattle or knocking noise from the supercharger at idle. While not catastrophic, it's an annoying and increasingly common issue on higher-mileage V6 XEs.
problems.recommendation: If your V6 XE develops a rattle from the supercharger at idle, have the coupler inspected immediately. A £500–£900 coupler replacement is far cheaper than the £900–£1,600 supercharger rebuild that follows if the coupler fails completely.

replacementCosts.h2: X760 Engine Replacement Costs — By Engine and Condition
replacementCosts.note: No Generation variance note required — no Engine Page baseline exists for these engines yet. Costs are sourced from UK specialist suppliers [JAG-QUOTE].

coreVariants.scopeNote: All variants listed are Jaguar XE X760 only. The XE is a single-generation model with no siblings in this generation.

mostRequestedEngines: 1. 2.0 Ingenium Diesel (largest share of 590 JLR‑wide figure)
2. 3.0 V6 Supercharged (largest share of 560 JLR‑wide figure)
3. 2.0 Ingenium Petrol (largest share of 500 JLR‑wide figure)
mostCommonFailures: 2. 3.0 V6 water pump and coolant pipes
